chapter_title.en: Chapter on Declaring the Intention (ihlal) for a Nullified Umrah, Its Termination, and Its Rites

text: 2944 - رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «إِذَا دَخَلَ اَلْمُعْتَمِرُ مَكَّةَ مِنْ غَيْرِ تَمَتُّعٍ وَ طَافَ بِالْبَيْتِ وَ صَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ عِنْدَ مَقَامِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ وَ سَعَى بَيْنَ اَلصَّفَا وَ اَلْمَرْوَةِ فَلْيَلْحَقْ بِأَهْلِهِ إِنْ شَاءَ ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2944 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"If an Mu'tamir (one performing Umrah) enters Mecca without intending Tamattu‘, performs Tawaf of the House, prays two rak'ahs at the Maqam of Ibrahim (as), and performs Sa'i between Safa and Marwah, he may return to his family if he wishes."
